summary: Gruber recounts his hands-on experience capturing spatial video on iPhone 15 Pro and viewing it on Vision Pro. Spatial video capture is simple—a toggle in the Camera app—but limited to 1080p/30fps due to the ultra-wide lens crop. The viewing experience on Vision Pro is astonishing: spatial videos feel like watching memories through a dream-like portal, not like watching a screen. He argues the iPhone will be the 'best' spatial video camera because it's always with you, even if Vision Pro captures technically superior footage. He also discovers that panoramic photos, long dismissed as a gimmick, become breathtaking on Vision Pro with zero optical distortion. His overall enthusiasm for Vision Pro as a platform has never been higher.
key_points: Spatial video capture on iPhone 15 Pro is limited to 1080p/30fps because cropping the ultra-wide sensor to match the main lens field of view doesn't yield enough pixels for 4K
Spatial videos viewed on Vision Pro feel like memories rather than recordings—dream-like, with hazy borders and a film-like quality that the 1080p/30fps limitations actually enhance
The iPhone will be the best spatial video camera despite Vision Pro's technical advantages, because the best camera is the one you have with you
Panoramic photos, previously a dismissed gimmick on flat displays, become breathtaking on Vision Pro with no optical distortion—like standing where the photo was taken
Vision Pro's foveated rendering is so fast that you can barely catch the resolution shifting even when darting your eyes rapidly
money_quotes: It's somehow simultaneously very natural-looking and utterly uncanny.
It's like watching — and listening to — a dream, through a hazy-bordered portal opened into another world.
Nothing you've ever viewed on a screen, however, can prepare you for the experience of watching these spatial videos, especially the ones you will have shot yourself, of your own family and friends.
They truly are more like memories than videos.
The best camera is the one you have with you. I have my iPhone with me almost everywhere. That will never be even close to true for Vision Pro next year.
Panoramic photos viewed using Vision Pro are breathtaking.
As a basic rule, going forward, I plan to capture spatial videos of people, especially my family and dearest friends, and panoramic photos of places I visit. It's like teleportation.
My enthusiasm for this platform, and the future of spatial computing, could not be higher.
key_insight: Spatial video on Vision Pro transforms ordinary iPhone footage into something that feels less like watching a recording and more like reliving a memory, making the always-with-you iPhone the most important spatial camera despite its technical limitations.
